description: Authoring-role profile for goal-spec Stage 1 OpenSpec work. Roles declare abstract modelClass values only; concrete provider/model ids are resolved by downstream harness bindings.

collector.purpose: Collect and cite source truth without deciding value, scope, or final wording.
responsibilities: Read source-manifest.json first for existing changes, then read only authoritative sources listed there.
Extract source-grounded claims, constraints, risks, validation signals, and open questions.
Label assumptions explicitly and keep source citations attached to load-bearing claims.

judge.purpose: Evaluate value, scope, no-build/smaller-scope alternatives, and archive/review readiness.
responsibilities: Challenge low-value, over-scoped, under-evidenced, or boundary-violating requests.
Decide whether the value gate is pass, no-build, smaller-scope, ask-before-drafting, or proceed_with_assumptions.
Require source-grounded rationale and explicit assumptions for any accepted uncertainty.
mustNot: Collect new source claims after judging without routing them back through collector evidence.
Write final OpenSpec prose as the same role that made the value decision.

writer.purpose: Write proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md, and specs/**/spec.md from the approved kernel and cited evidence.
responsibilities: Preserve the value-gate outcome, spec kernel, constraints, non-goals, and success signal.
Keep requirements normative, testable, and source-grounded.
Refresh source-manifest.json after authoritative sources change.
mustNot: Override the judge's value decision silently.
Put DAG runtime fields, model routing, scheduling, or worktree strategy into OpenSpec sources.

explainer.purpose: Produce the non-authoritative decision-review change-explainer.html from authoritative OpenSpec sources.
responsibilities: Read source-manifest.json before generating the explainer.
Represent decisions, risks, verification, and implementation slices without contradicting markdown/spec sources.
Run the strict explainer validator when a decision-review explainer is required.
mustNot: Introduce new requirements, promises, risks, or migrations absent from authoritative sources.

reviewer.purpose: Review the OpenSpec package for preservation, boundary fit, validation readiness, and archive readiness.
responsibilities: Check that load-bearing collector claims landed in authoritative OpenSpec sources or explicit open questions/non-goals.
Verify source-manifest freshness and explainer fidelity when applicable.
Block archive/readiness claims when unchecked tasks, stale manifests, invalid explainers, or unsupported evidence remain.
mustNot: Rewrite source truth while reviewing unless explicitly asked to make fixes.
Accept concrete provider/model ids in authoring profile or OpenSpec routing guidance.

separationRules.description: collector and judge are separate roles; evidence collection must not be the same role as value judgment.
judge decides whether/what to draft; writer turns an approved kernel into OpenSpec sources without silently expanding scope.
explainer writes only the review companion; markdown/spec sources remain authoritative.
reviewer performs the final preservation/readiness check after writer/explainer work.

sourceTruthRules: For existing OpenSpec changes, read source-manifest.json first and then the authoritative sources it lists.
Authoritative sources are proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md, and specs/**/spec.md.
change-explainer.html and .goal-spec/ workflow artifacts are non-authoritative.
Do not invent validators; use acceptance criteria when sources lack deterministic shell commands.
